insight.headline: Storefront API Update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive can be used to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel, such as online store or point of sale.
insight.developer_impact: Developers using the Storefront API should update their queries to include the channelId argument when necessary. This will ensure that their apps are fetching the correct data for the specific sales channel. Additionally, developers should review the Storefront API documentation to understand the implications of this change on their existing code. They should also test their apps to ensure that they are working as expected with the updated API.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants will benefit from more accurate and sales channel-specific data in their Storefront API-powered apps. This change will enable developers to build more robust and sales channel-aware apps, which will improve the overall merchant experience.
insight.why_it_matters: This update provides more flexibility and control over the data fetched from the Storefront API, allowing developers to build more tailored and sales channel-specific experiences for merchants. It also demonstrates Shopify's commitment to continually improving and expanding the capabilities of the Storefront API.

also_worth_knowing.text: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, allowing developers to build and test against new inventory APIs.
CLI version 4.5.2 fixes a warning when running theme commands in valid theme directories and bumps Shopify/theme-tools packages.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2 adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts in hydrogen upgrade.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: App Store requirements have been updated to include using a unique name for apps to reduce merchant confusion.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API @inContext directive now supports an optional channelId argument to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: The ExchangeLineItem type now includes productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle fields.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components, allowing apps to access component-level discount allocations.
CLI version 4.5.0 introduces new store commands, including listing and store creation.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1
updates.summary: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1 reads disabled command metadata and preserves actionable build errors during h2 deploy.

updates.title: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38
updates.summary: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38 adds an explicit ~ app alias to new Hydrogen projects and uses jsconfig.json for JavaScript projects.

recap.headline: Updates to GraphQL Admin API, Hydrogen, and Storefront API with new features and requirements

themes.title: New Features and Updates
themes.detail: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, and the Storefront API now supports the `channelId` argument in the `@inContext` directive. Additionally, new fields have been added to the `ExchangeLineItem` type, and POS UI Extensions now include discount allocations to bundle components.

themes.title: Hydrogen Updates
themes.detail: New versions of `@shopify/cli-hydrogen` and `@shopify/create-hydrogen` have been released with patch changes, including the addition of non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags, and the preservation of actionable build errors during deployment.

themes.title: App Store Requirements and Security
themes.detail: A new App Store requirement has been added, requiring apps to use a unique name, and the card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate. The `@shopify/cli` has also been updated to version 4.5.2 with bug fixes and improvements.

actions.text: Enable the physical inventory feature preview on a development store
Update apps to use a unique name to comply with the new App Store requirement
Obtain a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate for the card deposit endpoint
